title: Designing Data-Intensive Applications
subtitle: The Big Ideas Behind Reliable, Scalable, and Maintainable Systems
author: Martin Kleppmann
themes: Reliability, scalability, and maintainability as core design principles
Understanding trade-offs in distributed data systems
The evolution from SQL to NoSQL and back
Consistency models and their practical implications
excerpt: The definitive guide to the fundamental principles behind modern data systems — covering reliability, scalability, maintainability, and the distributed systems research that powers today's databases, message queues, and stream processors.
keyIdeas: Reliability, scalability, and maintainability as foundational properties
Storage engines — B-trees vs. LSM-trees and their trade-offs
keyTakeaways: Most applications are data-bound, not CPU-bound — understand your storage and access patterns
There is no free lunch in distributed systems — every choice involves trade-offs
Design for failure from day one, not as an afterthought
whoShouldSkip: Beginners without database or web application experience
